name: Webhook Debugger
description: Webhook delivery log with payload inspection, header viewing, status timeline, and retry controls for diagnosing integration failures.

standard.description: Full delivery log with expandable detail panel showing payload, headers, and status timeline per delivery

compact.description: Dense table-only view with status badges and inline retry buttons — no expandable detail, optimized for high-volume scanning

live.description: Streaming log that auto-scrolls to the latest delivery as webhooks arrive in real time with slide-in animation and a pulsing live indicator

visual_brief: Table listing webhook deliveries with columns: timestamp (muted monospace), endpoint URL (truncated with tooltip on hover), event type as a small pill label (e.g. 'invoice.paid', 'customer.created'), status badge (delivered = green filled pill with checkmark, failed = red filled pill with X icon, pending = yellow outlined pill with spinner), response time in milliseconds (green if <500ms, yellow if 500-2000ms, red if >2000ms), and a retry icon button on the far right. Each row is separated by a 1px border. Hovering a row highlights it with a subtle surface tint. Clicking a row expands an inline detail panel below it: the panel has three tabbed sections. First tab 'Request' shows request headers as a two-column key-value table and request body as syntax-highlighted JSON with line numbers in a monospace code block. Second tab 'Response' shows response status code as a large colored badge (2xx green, 4xx yellow, 5xx red), response headers table, and response body (syntax-highlighted). Third tab 'Timeline' shows a horizontal status timeline: sent timestamp with arrow, received timestamp with arrow, processed timestamp, each connected by a thin line with duration labels above. Failed deliveries have a red-tinted background row (rgba red at 0.05). The EndpointFilter sits above the table as a dropdown or multi-select chip row. Live preset adds a green pulsing dot labeled 'Live' in the top-right corner, and new rows slide in from the top with a fade-slide animation and briefly flash with an accent highlight before settling.

slots.filter-bar: A row above the table containing the EndpointFilter dropdown (multi-select for filtering by endpoint URL), an event type filter dropdown, a status filter (all/delivered/failed/pending toggle chips), and a date range picker. Active filters show as removable chips below the filter row.
slots.delivery-table: The main data table with a sticky header row showing column labels: Timestamp, Endpoint, Event, Status, Response Time, Actions. Each DeliveryRow renders one webhook delivery as a table row. Rows are sorted by timestamp descending (newest first). The table supports infinite scroll or pagination at the bottom. Alternating row backgrounds at very subtle opacity for scannability.
slots.delivery-detail: An expandable panel that appears inline below a clicked DeliveryRow. Contains three tabs: Request (headers table + JSON body viewer), Response (status badge + headers + body), and Timeline (horizontal step indicator showing sent/received/processed with timestamps and durations). The panel has a slightly inset background and a colored left border matching the delivery status. A 'Retry' button sits in the top-right corner of the detail panel. Close button (X) in the top-right dismisses the detail.

responsive.mobile: Table switches to card layout — each delivery renders as a stacked card showing status badge, event type, endpoint (truncated), and timestamp. Tap opens detail as a bottom sheet. Filter bar collapses to a single filter icon button that opens a drawer.
responsive.tablet: Table layout maintained with horizontal scroll if needed. Detail panel opens inline below the row. Filter bar fully visible.
responsive.desktop: Full table with all columns visible without scroll. Detail panel opens inline with comfortable width. Filter bar displays all controls in a single row.

micro.retry-press: scale(0.95) 100ms ease-in + rotate(360deg) 600ms ease-out on trigger

composition.DeliveryRow: Row(d-interactive, hover-highlight, status-tint: failed→red) > [Timestamp(mono-data) + Endpoint(truncate) + EventType(d-annotation, pill) + StatusBadge(d-annotation, color: status) + ResponseTime(mono-data, color: threshold) + RetryButton(d-interactive, icon-only)]
composition.DeliveryDetail: Panel(d-surface, inset, border-left: status-color, tabbed) > [RequestTab > [HeadersTable(d-data) + PayloadViewer(d-data, syntax-highlight: json)] + ResponseTab > [StatusCode(d-annotation, large) + HeadersTable + PayloadViewer] + TimelineTab > StatusTimeline(d-data, horizontal-steps)]

layout_hints.delivery_priority: Delivery state and endpoint identity should be visible before expanded payload detail. The row list is the primary operational surface; request/response panes are secondary inspection layers.
layout_hints.detail_paneling: Expanded request, response, and timeline views should remain clearly tied to the active delivery row so debugging context is never lost.
layout_hints.live_mode: When new deliveries stream in, preserve row stability and status visibility rather than letting the list jump around unpredictably.

accessibility.aria-label: Webhook delivery log with payload inspection
keyboard: Arrow Up/Down: navigate between delivery rows
Enter: expand/collapse delivery detail for the focused row
Tab: move between detail tabs (Request, Response, Timeline)
announcements: Detail panel expanded for delivery at {timestamp}
